description: Production deployment generation with full security, autoscaling, probes, and resource management
input: Create a production-ready Kubernetes Deployment for a Node.js API with autoscaling, health probes, resource limits, and proper security context

description: Helm chart generation with multi-environment values and dependencies
input: Generate a Helm chart for a microservice that needs Redis and PostgreSQL, with separate values for staging and production
assertions: Chart.yaml with Redis and PostgreSQL dependencies

description: Security review catches privileged container and missing NetworkPolicy
input: Review my deployment for security issues: it runs as root, has no resource limits, and I'm not sure about the network setup
assertions: Identifies missing resource limits as FM-2

description: GitOps workflow with Flux CD for a multi-environment setup
input: Set up GitOps for my Kubernetes cluster using Flux CD. I have staging and production environments with different configs

description: Diagnose and fix a crashing pod with OOMKilled
input: fix pod: my-api-pod keeps restarting with OOMKilled in production. It's a Java service with default settings
assertions: Recommends memory limits increase or JVM heap tuning
Recommends HPA based on actual metrics

description: Multi-cloud deployment with EKS-specific IRSA and spot instances
input: deploy to EKS with spot instances for cost savings, need IRSA for S3 access, and TLS termination via cert-manager

description: NEAR-MISS NEGATIVE: Docker-only question without Kubernetes context
input: How do I optimize my Dockerfile to use multi-stage builds and reduce image size?
assertions: This is a Docker/pure container question without K8s deployment context

description: NEAR-MISS NEGATIVE: Terraform infrastructure provisioning without K8s context
input: Write Terraform to provision an RDS instance, VPC, and subnets for my application
assertions: This is infrastructure-as-code (Terraform) without Kubernetes deployment context — use infrastructure-as-code skill
